mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market expansion and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2013 payroll growth of 165k indicates sustained labor market recovery
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with sub-5% unemployment target
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remaining at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) maintains an accommodative monetary stance that suppresses discount rates and sustains equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2013 FEDFUNDS at 0.15% reflects continued post-crisis accommodation
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns becomes significantly negative (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer and construction demand, which when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through multiplier effects on corporate revenues.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show positive month-over-month growth in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:March 2013 HOUST at 1036k and RSAFS at 419b show concurrent demand strength
failure_condition: Months with simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST growth produce negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above its 3-month moving average indicates accelerating supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds its 3-month moving average by more than 0.5 index points
evidence_refs: fact:April 2013 INDPRO at 98.74 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 3-month MA produce positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month period signals tightening labor market conditions and wage pressure, which reduces corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2pp over the preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:April 2013 UNRATE at 7.5% down from prior months indicates labor tightening
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with wage acceleration risk
failure_condition: Periods of rapid UNRATE decline produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or the correlation between 3-month UNRATE change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year, when combined with accommodative FEDFUNDS near zero, creates a real negative interest rate regime that erodes fixed-income returns and forces equity risk premium compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the inflation-real rate mismatch becomes unsustainable.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:March 2013 CPIAUCSL at 232.34 with FEDFUNDS at 0.15% creates negative real rate
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Periods meeting the trigger condition produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors extend equity duration in a growth regime.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2013 GDPC1 at 13750.1b reflects post-recession expansion
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2.5% produce negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction of more than 0.3 index points month-over-month, when occurring in a regime where PAYEMS growth has slowed below 100k, signals synchronized supply and labor weakness that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk repricing occurs.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 0.3 points month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth falls below 100k in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:April 2013 INDPRO at 98.74 and PAYEMS at 135474k show no joint weakness signal
regime_hint: synchronized supply-labor contraction with recession signals
failure_condition: Months meeting both trigger conditions produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.
